text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland Richard Payne and Matt Norris Fifth Sunday of Easter May 3 2026 This weekend May 2nd 3rd the Church celebrates the Fifth Sunday of Easter As Eastertide continues our Gospel readings begin a narrative shift Having focused on the Resurrection and the Lord's appearances to the Apostles these many weeks we now begin to prepare for His coming Ascension Christ does not ascend to abandon His disciples but to draw them upward with Him He ascends so that His Body the Church might follow Even now the Church lives with Her eyes fixed toward Heaven Each Mass lifts us into the heavenly worship Each sacrament draws us deeper into that divine life opened to us by the Resurrection Easter is not simply the memory of the empty tomb but the very beginning of our own ascent in Christ We are being reminded that our true homeland is not here below but above in heaven where Christ is seated at the right hand of the Father Even still our Easter celebration does not end with the Ascension Instead it continues through the Ascension to the Solemnity of Pentecost Just as the Risen Lord opened the gates of Heaven through His victory over death and ascended before His disciples so too does He send the Holy Spirit to strengthen and guide His Church until the end of time The same Spirit given at Pentecost continues to animate His Church today Celebration of First Holy Communion This joyous transition in the liturgical year from Easter to the Ascension and finally to the Solemnity of Pentecost is a mystery that continues to unfold within the life of the Church today Through the gift of the Sacraments Christ continues to draw His people into communion with Himself nourishing and strengthening us along the journey toward Heaven Most especially He nourishes through that very gift of Himself that is the Most Holy Eucharist This weekend we congratulate our six young parishioners who will receive their First Holy Communion at our 10 AM Mass They will for the very first time come forward to receive the Body Blood Soul and Divinity of Jesus Christ in the Most Holy Eucharist As they receive Our Lord for the first time they will be drawn ever more deeply into the very mystery of Easter that we continue to celebrate throughout this holy season Please continue to keep these six and their families in your prayers We ask our Lord that their love for Him in the Holy Eucharist may continue to grow throughout their lives and that they may always remain close to Him who feeds us with the Bread of Heaven From the witness of their joy may we also find in our hearts a renewal of this wonder and joy as we too approach this same altar and receive our Lord Himself in Holy Communion Renovation Update and General Practices Though construction continues in the Sanctuary I hope that these past two weekends have proven to be an easy transition back into the Nave or main body of the Church Once the Sanctuary itself is usable we will install the last two rows of pews in the front of the Nave This will not only help to increase our overall seating capacity but also expand our accessible seating options As of this past week it does seem prudent to move our completion date from Pentecost Sunday May 24th to June Though work is moving along at a good pace we have faced a number of delays on receiving materials We are still waiting the arrival of the marble for the Sanctuary and the majority of a needed woodworking package from Big Lumber Once these materials have arrived our team from Shawnee Construction is ready to start installation Keeping an Eye on the Calendar As a reminder our Parish Calendar is available in both our parish bulletin and online on our parish website Please keep an eye on the calendar over the next few weeks as both Michelle in our Parish Office and I will be making use of some vacation days This means that we will occasionally have altered Parish Office Hours or daily Mass times as listed on the calendar Saturday May 2 8:00am Special Intention 830 9:00am Confession 400 4:45pm
